1	/* SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT
2	 *
3	 * Copyright (C) 2017-2023 WireGuard LLC. All Rights Reserved.
4	 */
5	
6	package device
7	
8	import (
9		"container/list"
10		"errors"
11		"sync"
12		"sync/atomic"
13		"time"
14	
15		"github.com/amnezia-vpn/amneziawg-go/conn"
16		"github.com/amnezia-vpn/amneziawg-go/device/awg"
17	)
18	
19	type Peer struct {
20		isRunning         atomic.Bool
21		keypairs          Keypairs
22		handshake         Handshake
23		device            *Device
24		stopping          sync.WaitGroup // routines pending stop
25		txBytes           atomic.Uint64  // bytes send to peer (endpoint)
26		rxBytes           atomic.Uint64  // bytes received from peer
27		lastHandshakeNano atomic.Int64   // nano seconds since epoch
28	
29		endpoint struct {
30			sync.Mutex
31			val            conn.Endpoint
32			clearSrcOnTx   bool // signal to val.ClearSrc() prior to next packet transmission
33			disableRoaming bool
34		}
35	
36		timers struct {
37			retransmitHandshake     *Timer
38			sendKeepalive           *Timer
39			newHandshake            *Timer
40			zeroKeyMaterial         *Timer
41			persistentKeepalive     *Timer
42			handshakeAttempts       atomic.Uint32
43			needAnotherKeepalive    atomic.Bool
44			sentLastMinuteHandshake atomic.Bool
45		}
46	
47		state struct {
48			sync.Mutex // protects against concurrent Start/Stop
49		}
50	
51		queue struct {
52			staged   chan *QueueOutboundElementsContainer // staged packets before a handshake is available
53			outbound *autodrainingOutboundQueue           // sequential ordering of udp transmission
54			inbound  *autodrainingInboundQueue            // sequential ordering of tun writing
55		}
56	
57		cookieGenerator             CookieGenerator
58		trieEntries                 list.List
59		persistentKeepaliveInterval atomic.Uint32
60	}
61	
62	func (device *Device) NewPeer(pk NoisePublicKey) (*Peer, error) {
63		if device.isClosed() {
64			return nil, errors.New("device closed")
65		}
66	
67		// lock resources
68		device.staticIdentity.RLock()
69		defer device.staticIdentity.RUnlock()
70	
71		device.peers.Lock()
72		defer device.peers.Unlock()
73	
74		// check if over limit
75		if len(device.peers.keyMap) >= MaxPeers {
76			return nil, errors.New("too many peers")
77		}
78	
79		// create peer
80		peer := new(Peer)
81	
82		peer.cookieGenerator.Init(pk)
83		peer.device = device
84		peer.queue.outbound = newAutodrainingOutboundQueue(device)
85		peer.queue.inbound = newAutodrainingInboundQueue(device)
86		peer.queue.staged = make(chan *QueueOutboundElementsContainer, QueueStagedSize)
87	
88		// map public key
89		_, ok := device.peers.keyMap[pk]
90		if ok {
91			return nil, errors.New("adding existing peer")
92		}
93	
94		// pre-compute DH
95		handshake := &peer.handshake
96		handshake.mutex.Lock()
97		handshake.precomputedStaticStatic, _ = device.staticIdentity.privateKey.sharedSecret(pk)
98		handshake.remoteStatic = pk
99		handshake.mutex.Unlock()
100	
101		// reset endpoint
102		peer.endpoint.Lock()
103		peer.endpoint.val = nil
104		peer.endpoint.disableRoaming = false
105		peer.endpoint.clearSrcOnTx = false
106		peer.endpoint.Unlock()
107	
108		// init timers
109		peer.timersInit()
110	
111		// add
112		device.peers.keyMap[pk] = peer
113	
114		return peer, nil
115	}
116	
117	func (peer *Peer) SendBuffers(buffers [][]byte) error {
118		peer.device.net.RLock()
119		defer peer.device.net.RUnlock()
120	
121		if peer.device.isClosed() {
122			return nil
123		}
124	
125		peer.endpoint.Lock()
126		endpoint := peer.endpoint.val
127		if endpoint == nil {
128			peer.endpoint.Unlock()
129			return errors.New("no known endpoint for peer")
130		}
131		if peer.endpoint.clearSrcOnTx {
132			endpoint.ClearSrc()
133			peer.endpoint.clearSrcOnTx = false
134		}
135		peer.endpoint.Unlock()
136	
137		err := peer.device.net.bind.Send(buffers, endpoint)
138		if err == nil {
139			var totalLen uint64
140			for _, b := range buffers {
141				totalLen += uint64(len(b))
142			}
143			peer.txBytes.Add(totalLen)
144		}
145		return err
146	}
147	
148	func (peer *Peer) SendAndCountBuffers(buffers [][]byte) error {
149		err := peer.SendBuffers(buffers)
150		if err == nil {
151			awg.PacketCounter.Add(uint64(len(buffers)))
152			return nil
153		}
154	
155		return err
156	}
157	
158	func (peer *Peer) String() string {
159		// The awful goo that follows is identical to:
160		//
161		//   base64Key := base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(peer.handshake.remoteStatic[:])
162		//   abbreviatedKey := base64Key[0:4] + "…" + base64Key[39:43]
163		//   return fmt.Sprintf("peer(%s)", abbreviatedKey)
164		//
165		// except that it is considerably more efficient.
166		src := peer.handshake.remoteStatic
167		b64 := func(input byte) byte {
168			return input + 'A' + byte(((25-int(input))>>8)&6) - byte(((51-int(input))>>8)&75) - byte(((61-int(input))>>8)&15) + byte(((62-int(input))>>8)&3)
169		}
170		b := []byte("peer(____…____)")
171		const first = len("peer(")
172		const second = len("peer(____…")
173		b[first+0] = b64((src[0] >> 2) & 63)
174		b[first+1] = b64(((src[0] << 4) | (src[1] >> 4)) & 63)
175		b[first+2] = b64(((src[1] << 2) | (src[2] >> 6)) & 63)
176		b[first+3] = b64(src[2] & 63)
177		b[second+0] = b64(src[29] & 63)
178		b[second+1] = b64((src[30] >> 2) & 63)
179		b[second+2] = b64(((src[30] << 4) | (src[31] >> 4)) & 63)
180		b[second+3] = b64((src[31] << 2) & 63)
181		return string(b)
182	}
183	
184	func (peer *Peer) Start() {
185		// should never start a peer on a closed device
186		if peer.device.isClosed() {
187			return
188		}
189	
190		// prevent simultaneous start/stop operations
191		peer.state.Lock()
192		defer peer.state.Unlock()
193	
194		if peer.isRunning.Load() {
195			return
196		}
197	
198		device := peer.device
199		device.log.Verbosef("%v - Starting", peer)
200	
201		// reset routine state
202		peer.stopping.Wait()
203		peer.stopping.Add(2)
204	
205		peer.handshake.mutex.Lock()
206		peer.handshake.lastSentHandshake = time.Now().Add(-(RekeyTimeout + time.Second))
207		peer.handshake.mutex.Unlock()
208	
209		peer.device.queue.encryption.wg.Add(1) // keep encryption queue open for our writes
210	
211		peer.timersStart()
212	
213		device.flushInboundQueue(peer.queue.inbound)
214		device.flushOutboundQueue(peer.queue.outbound)
215	
216		// Use the device batch size, not the bind batch size, as the device size is
217		// the size of the batch pools.
218		batchSize := peer.device.BatchSize()
219		go peer.RoutineSequentialSender(batchSize)
220		go peer.RoutineSequentialReceiver(batchSize)
221	
222		peer.isRunning.Store(true)
223	}
224	
225	func (peer *Peer) ZeroAndFlushAll() {
226		device := peer.device
227	
228		// clear key pairs
229	
230		keypairs := &peer.keypairs
231		keypairs.Lock()
232		device.DeleteKeypair(keypairs.previous)
233		device.DeleteKeypair(keypairs.current)
234		device.DeleteKeypair(keypairs.next.Load())
235		keypairs.previous = nil
236		keypairs.current = nil
237		keypairs.next.Store(nil)
238		keypairs.Unlock()
239	
240		// clear handshake state
241	
242		handshake := &peer.handshake
243		handshake.mutex.Lock()
244		device.indexTable.Delete(handshake.localIndex)
245		handshake.Clear()
246		handshake.mutex.Unlock()
247	
248		peer.FlushStagedPackets()
249	}
250	
251	func (peer *Peer) ExpireCurrentKeypairs() {
252		handshake := &peer.handshake
253		handshake.mutex.Lock()
254		peer.device.indexTable.Delete(handshake.localIndex)
255		handshake.Clear()
256		peer.handshake.lastSentHandshake = time.Now().Add(-(RekeyTimeout + time.Second))
257		handshake.mutex.Unlock()
258	
259		keypairs := &peer.keypairs
260		keypairs.Lock()
261		if keypairs.current != nil {
262			keypairs.current.sendNonce.Store(RejectAfterMessages)
263		}
264		if next := keypairs.next.Load(); next != nil {
265			next.sendNonce.Store(RejectAfterMessages)
266		}
267		keypairs.Unlock()
268	}
269	
270	func (peer *Peer) Stop() {
271		peer.state.Lock()
272		defer peer.state.Unlock()
273	
274		if !peer.isRunning.Swap(false) {
275			return
276		}
277	
278		peer.device.log.Verbosef("%v - Stopping", peer)
279	
280		peer.timersStop()
281		// Signal that RoutineSequentialSender and RoutineSequentialReceiver should exit.
282		peer.queue.inbound.c <- nil
283		peer.queue.outbound.c <- nil
284		peer.stopping.Wait()
285		peer.device.queue.encryption.wg.Done() // no more writes to encryption queue from us
286	
287		peer.ZeroAndFlushAll()
288	}
289	
290	func (peer *Peer) SetEndpointFromPacket(endpoint conn.Endpoint) {
291		peer.endpoint.Lock()
292		defer peer.endpoint.Unlock()
293		if peer.endpoint.disableRoaming {
294			return
295		}
296		peer.endpoint.clearSrcOnTx = false
297		peer.endpoint.val = endpoint
298	}
299	
300	func (peer *Peer) markEndpointSrcForClearing() {
301		peer.endpoint.Lock()
302		defer peer.endpoint.Unlock()
303		if peer.endpoint.val == nil {
304			return
305		}
306		peer.endpoint.clearSrcOnTx = true
307	}

395		var nextIdx int

397			nextIdx = 0
398		} else {
399			nextIdx = (prev + 1) % total
400		}

402		h.activeSpec = nextSpec
403		h.activeAddr = next
404		h.idx = nextIdx
405		h.lastSwitch = now
406	
407		// Repoint the DATA/PROBE plane FIRST so probes immediately start re-arming detection
408		// against the new endpoint, then initiate the fresh WG re-handshake toward it.
409		h.remote.SetPeerRemote(next)
410		if h.rehandshake != nil {
411			h.rehandshake()
412		}
413		h.log.Warn("hub failover: all paths to active concentrator down; switched endpoint and re-handshaked",
414			"from_index", prev, "to_index", nextIdx, "to_spec", nextSpec, "to_endpoint", next.String(), "endpoints", total)
415	}
416	
417	// deviceRehandshake returns a rehandshake bound to the engine peer identified by pk: it
418	// EXPIRES the peer's current keypairs (dropping the old hub's session — a fresh session
419	// with NO hub-to-hub state handoff) and sends a fresh handshake initiation, which the
420	// Bind fans out to the just-repointed standby endpoint. All hubs in the ordered set
421	// share the peer's single WireGuard static key, so the SAME peer identity re-handshakes
422	// against whichever hub is now active. A peer the engine cannot resolve (never configured
423	// — impossible for the edge's sole peer) yields a no-op so the loop never dereferences
424	// nil. This is the ONLY engine-peer coupling the failover path takes; it stays in the
425	// device package alongside the rest of the engine wiring.
426	func deviceRehandshake(dev *awgdevice.Device, pk config.Key) rehandshake {
427		key := awgdevice.NoisePublicKey(pk.Bytes())
428		return func() {
429			peer := dev.LookupPeer(key)
430			if peer == nil {
431				return
432			}
433			// Drop the current session so the re-handshake starts a FRESH keypair (no hub-to-hub
434			// handoff); ExpireCurrentKeypairs also backdates lastSentHandshake so the immediately
435			// following initiation is not suppressed by the RekeyTimeout guard.
436			peer.ExpireCurrentKeypairs()
437			_ = peer.SendHandshakeInitiation(false)
438		}
439	}

22	// T60 is the netns e2e for the W1 startup-resilience feature END TO END: T51's
23	// tolerant Open() (defer a well-formed but not-yet-assignable source_addr instead of
24	// refusing the whole bond) composed with T55's background reconcile (promote a
25	// deferred path to live once its address appears), plus the two fast-fail edges
26	// (zero bindable paths; a malformed source_addr) that tolerance must NOT paper over.
27	
28	const (
29		// t60MetricsListen is the edge's /metrics endpoint for this file's tests, on a
30		// port none of the other e2e files use (see test/e2e/p2/p3/p4/pacing for 9095-97).
31		t60MetricsListen = "127.0.0.1:9098"
32		t60MetricsURL    = "http://" + t60MetricsListen + "/metrics"
33	
34		// t60UnassignableSrcA/B mirror internal/bind/tolerant_bind_test.go's
35		// unassignableSource: 192.0.2.0/24 is TEST-NET-1 (RFC 5737), reserved for
36		// documentation and guaranteed never configured on a real interface, so a bind
37		// against it deterministically fails EADDRNOTAVAIL without any netns fixture.
38		t60UnassignableSrcA = "192.0.2.1"
39		t60UnassignableSrcB = "192.0.2.2"
40	)
41	
42	// t60DeferredPath is the THIRD emulated uplink (distinct veth names from
43	// DefaultPaths' starlink/cellular): its edge address is deliberately withheld at
44	// Setup (deferEdgeAddr), so wbCe exists and is up but 10.100.3.1 is not yet owned by
45	// any interface — the well-formed-but-not-yet-assignable condition T51 defers.
46	// top.AddEdgeAddr("modem5g") later adds it, simulating the address appearing.
47	var t60DeferredPath = pathSpec{
48		name: "modem5g", edgeIP: "10.100.3.1", concIP: "10.100.3.2",
49		edgeVeth: "wbCe", concVeth: "wbCc", delayMs: 20,
50		deferEdgeAddr: true,
51	}
52	
53	// t60PromoteTimeout bounds how long the T55 background reconcile may take to bind,
54	// promote, and bring the deferred path's liveness to Up after its address appears:
55	// one worst-case reconcile tick (bind.DefaultReconcileInterval, the poll cadence)
56	// plus the liveness up-transition (DefaultUpSuccesses consecutive probe echoes at
57	// DefaultProbeInterval), plus harness slack for scheduling jitter under the fixture's
58	// shared CPU — mirroring the analytical-bound style of thresholds.go's
59	// PLivenessFailoverBudget/PLivenessDetectBudget.
60	const t60PromoteTimeout = bind.DefaultReconcileInterval +
61		time.Duration(telemetry.DefaultUpSuccesses)*telemetry.DefaultProbeInterval +
62		3*time.Second
63	
64	// TestTolerantStartupDeferredPathPromotes is the T60 acceptance: bring the bond up
65	// with TWO paths where one (modem5g) names a well-formed source_addr that is not yet
66	// assignable on any interface. The bond must come up on the survivor (starlink) —
67	// handshake + traffic — while modem5g is present in config but excluded from the
68	// live path set (not fatal). Adding the missing address must then let T55's
69	// background reconcile bind, promote, and carry traffic on modem5g.
70	func TestTolerantStartupDeferredPathPromotes(t *testing.T) {
71		bin := buildWanbond(t)
72		survivor := DefaultPaths[0] // starlink
73		paths := []pathSpec{survivor, t60DeferredPath}
74		top := SetupWithPaths(t, paths)
75		edge, conc := setupT60Tunnel(t, top, bin, paths, t60MetricsListen)
76	
77		// (1) The bond comes up on the survivor alone: modem5g's source_addr is not yet
78		// assignable, so its bind deferred rather than failing the whole Open (T51).
79		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
80			t.Fatalf("bond never came up on the survivor path %q while %q was deferred\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
81				survivor.name, t60DeferredPath.name, edge.log(), conc.log())
82		}
83		mbps := top.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3)
84		if mbps <= 0 {
85			t.Fatalf("survivor path %q carried non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", survivor.name, mbps)
86		}
87	
88		// The deferred path is PRESENT in config but excluded from the live/served set:
89		// bind.Multipath.PathSnapshots() — what the /metrics endpoint scrapes — reports
90		// only BOUND paths (m.paths), so a still-deferred path's wanbond_path_up series is
91		// simply ABSENT, not zero. This operationalizes "present-but-down, not fatal":
92		// the daemon is running, the survivor is up, and the deferred path has no live
93		// series until T55 promotes it.
94		before := scrapeMetrics(t, t60MetricsURL)
95		if _, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, t60DeferredPath.name); ok {
96			t.Fatalf("deferred path %q already has a wanbond_path_up series before its address was added; it should be absent (not yet promoted)", t60DeferredPath.name)
97		}
98		if v, ok := before.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, survivor.name); !ok || v != 1 {
99			t.Fatalf("survivor path %q wanbond_path_up = %v (present=%v), want 1", survivor.name, v, ok)
100		}
101		t.Logf("pre-promote: survivor %q up (iperf3=%.1f Mbit/s), deferred %q absent from live metrics as expected", survivor.name, mbps, t60DeferredPath.name)
102	
103		// (2) The missing address appears — e.g. the 5G modem's DHCP lease completes.
104		top.AddEdgeAddr(t60DeferredPath.name)
105	
106		// T55's background reconcile must bind, promote, and bring the path's liveness to
107		// Up within the analytical budget above.
108		waitPathUp(t, t60MetricsURL, t60DeferredPath.name, 1, t60PromoteTimeout)
109	
110		// Prove the PROMOTED path itself carries traffic (not merely that the bond
111		// survives): blackhole the survivor so only the reconciled path can forward. The
112		// active-backup scheduler's Pick can only land here once modem5g is genuinely
113		// admitted and healthy.
114		top.Blackhole(survivor.name)
115		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, time.Duration(P1RecoverySeconds)*time.Second+2*time.Second) {
116			t.Fatalf("tunnel did not recover on the promoted path %q once the survivor %q was blackholed\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
117				t60DeferredPath.name, survivor.name, edge.log(), conc.log())
118		}
119		promotedMbps := top.iperf3Mbps(t, concInner, 3)
120		if promotedMbps <= 0 {
121			t.Fatalf("promoted path %q carried non-positive throughput %.2f Mbit/s", t60DeferredPath.name, promotedMbps)
122		}
123		top.Restore(survivor.name)
124	
125		t.Logf("T60: survivor %q up first (iperf3=%.1f Mbit/s) while %q was deferred; after its address was added, T55 promoted it and it alone carried iperf3=%.1f Mbit/s",
126			survivor.name, mbps, t60DeferredPath.name, promotedMbps)
127	}

129	// TestTolerantStartupFastFailModes asserts the two edges tolerance must NOT paper
130	// over: zero bindable paths (every source_addr unassignable) is a FATAL Open, and a
131	// malformed source_addr is rejected at config LOAD, before the daemon ever attempts a
132	// bind. Both run in the test-process network namespace (the TestMain re-exec unshared
133	// it); neither needs the veth topology, since both source addresses are TEST-NET-1
134	// (RFC 5737, never assignable on any real interface) or simply invalid syntax.
135	func TestTolerantStartupFastFailModes(t *testing.T) {
136		bin := buildWanbond(t)
137	
138		t.Run("zero_bindable_paths_is_fatal", func(t *testing.T) {
139			// Determinism (hardware-robustness fix): the zero-bindable premise is that BOTH
140			// source_addrs fail to bind, i.e. a bind to a non-local address returns
141			// EADDRNOTAVAIL. On this kernel that rejection needs TWO conditions, BOTH pinned
142			// here so the premise holds regardless of host default or subtest order:
143			//   (1) at least one interface UP — with lo DOWN a non-local bind SUCCEEDS even at
144			//       ip_nonlocal_bind=0 (empirical kernel probe on llm-ubuntu-0); this subtest
145			//       builds no topology, so it must bring lo up itself (bringLoopbackUp);
146			//   (2) net.ipv4.ip_nonlocal_bind=0 — a host at 1 lets a non-local bind succeed
147			//       (disableNonlocalBind, which fails loudly if it cannot pin the value).
148			// Together they make "no configured path can bind -> fatal Open" deterministic.
149			// The original fix pinned only (2) and mis-identified the variable, so the daemon
150			// came up (both TEST-NET-1 addresses bound) and the test hung — the false-negative
151			// observed on llm-ubuntu-0 when this subtest ran alone (lo down).
152			bringLoopbackUp(t)
153			disableNonlocalBind(t)
154	
155			cfg := writeT60EdgeConfig(t, fmt.Sprintf(`[[paths]]
156	name = "a"
157	source_addr = "%s"
158	
159	[[paths]]
160	name = "b"
161	source_addr = "%s"
162	`, t60UnassignableSrcA, t60UnassignableSrcB))
163	
164			code, out := runWanbondOnce(t, bin, cfg, 10*time.Second)
165			if code == 0 {
166				t.Fatalf("wanbond exited 0 with zero bindable paths (both %s/%s are TEST-NET-1, never assignable); want a fatal non-zero exit\n%s",
167					t60UnassignableSrcA, t60UnassignableSrcB, out)
168			}
169			if !strings.Contains(out, "wanbond starting") {
170				t.Fatalf("expected the daemon to log startup before failing at Open — that is what distinguishes this fatal-Open case from a config-load error; output:\n%s", out)
171			}
172			if !strings.Contains(out, "no configured path could bind") {
173				t.Fatalf("expected the bind's zero-bindable fatal error message; output:\n%s", out)
174			}
175			t.Logf("zero-bindable-paths: wanbond exited %d as expected:\n%s", code, out)
176		})
177	
178		t.Run("malformed_source_addr_is_config_load_error", func(t *testing.T) {
179			cfg := writeT60EdgeConfig(t, `[[paths]]
180	name = "a"
181	source_addr = "not-an-ip-address"
182	`)
183	
184			code, out := runWanbondOnce(t, bin, cfg, 10*time.Second)
185			if code == 0 {
186				t.Fatalf("wanbond exited 0 with a malformed source_addr; want a config-load error\n%s", out)
187			}
188			if strings.Contains(out, "wanbond starting") {
189				t.Fatalf("a malformed source_addr must be rejected at config LOAD, before the daemon starts (the startup log should never appear); output:\n%s", out)
190			}
191			if !strings.Contains(out, "invalid source_addr") {
192				t.Fatalf("expected config.normalize's invalid-source_addr error; output:\n%s", out)
193			}
194			t.Logf("malformed-source_addr: wanbond exited %d at config load as expected:\n%s", code, out)
195		})
196	}
197	
198	// writeT60EdgeConfig writes a minimal, otherwise-valid edge config (role, psk,
199	// wireguard private/peer key, a bogus-but-syntactically-fine peer endpoint) with
200	// pathsBlock spliced in as the [[paths]] section(s) — the ONLY thing under test in
201	// TestTolerantStartupFastFailModes. Neither subtest reaches the peer, so the
202	// endpoint/allowed_ips values need not be routable.
203	//
204	// [log] level is "info" (NOT "error"): the zero-bindable subtest distinguishes a
205	// fatal Open() from a config-load failure by asserting the "wanbond starting" marker
206	// (main.Info, cmd/wanbond/main.go) IS present — that marker is logged at Info, so an
207	// "error" level would suppress it and the assertion could never hold (the daemon runs
208	// but logs nothing at Info). The malformed-source_addr subtest still sees NO
209	// "wanbond starting" because config load fails BEFORE that log, independent of level.
210	func writeT60EdgeConfig(t *testing.T, pathsBlock string) string {
211		t.Helper()
212		edgePriv, _ := genKey(t)
213		_, concPub := genKey(t)
214		psk := randKey(t)
215		dir := t.TempDir()
216		return writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
217	psk = "%s"
218	
219	%s
220	[wireguard]
221	private_key = "%s"
222	
223	[[wireguard.peers]]
224	public_key = "%s"
225	endpoint = "192.0.2.53:51820"
226	allowed_ips = ["10.10.0.2/32"]
227	
228	[log]
229	level = "info"
230	`, psk, pathsBlock, edgePriv, concPub))
231	}
232	
233	// runWanbondOnce runs bin --config cfgPath to completion — no signal is sent; the
234	// process must exit ON ITS OWN — within deadline, and returns its exit code and
235	// combined stdout+stderr. It fails the test if the process does not exit within the
236	// deadline: for these two fast-fail scenarios a hang would mean Open (or config
237	// load) blocked instead of failing fast, which is itself a defect worth surfacing
238	// loudly rather than silently killing the process and reporting a false pass.
239	func runWanbondOnce(t *testing.T, bin, cfgPath string, deadline time.Duration) (exitCode int, output string) {
240		t.Helper()
241		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), deadline)
242		defer cancel()
243		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, bin, "--config", cfgPath)
244		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
245		if ctx.Err() == context.DeadlineExceeded {
246			t.Fatalf("wanbond did not exit within %s (expected a fast fail); output so far:\n%s", deadline, out)
247		}
248		if err == nil {
249			return 0, string(out)
250		}
251		var ee *exec.ExitError
252		if errors.As(err, &ee) {
253			return ee.ExitCode(), string(out)
254		}
255		t.Fatalf("run wanbond --config %s: %v\n%s", cfgPath, err, out)
256		return -1, string(out)
257	}
258	
259	// setupT60Tunnel is setupMultipathTunnelLevel plus an edge [metrics] listen block:
260	// T60 needs to scrape the edge's per-path wanbond_path_up series to observe the
261	// deferred path being absent, then promoted. It otherwise mirrors
262	// setupMultipathTunnelLevel's config shape and bring-up sequence exactly.
263	func setupT60Tunnel(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, metricsListen string) (edge, conc *proc) {
264		t.Helper()
265	
266		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
267		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
268		psk := randKey(t)
269	
270		var edgePaths, concPaths strings.Builder
271		for _, p := range paths {
272			fmt.Fprintf(&edgePaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\ndest_addr = \"%s:%d\"\n\n", p.name, p.edgeIP, p.concIP, listenPort)
273			fmt.Fprintf(&concPaths, "[[paths]]\nname = %q\nsource_addr = %q\n\n", p.name, p.concIP)
274		}
275		// The wireguard peer endpoint (edge -> concentrator) seeds the virtual endpoint;
276		// the first configured path's concentrator address serves (mirrors
277		// setupMultipathTunnelLevel).
278		primary := paths[0]
279		metricsBlock := fmt.Sprintf("[metrics]\nlisten = %q\n\n", metricsListen)
280	
281		dir := t.TempDir()
282		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "edge"
283	psk = "%s"
284	
285	%s%s[wireguard]
286	private_key = "%s"
287	
288	[[wireguard.peers]]
289	public_key = "%s"
290	endpoint = "%s:%d"
291	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
292	
293	[log]
294	level = "error"
295	`, psk, edgePaths.String(), metricsBlock, edgePriv, concPub, primary.concIP, listenPort, concInner))
296	
297		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"), fmt.Sprintf(`role = "concentrator"
298	psk = "%s"
299	
300	%s[wireguard]
301	private_key = "%s"
302	listen_port = %d
303	
304	[[wireguard.peers]]
305	public_key = "%s"
306	allowed_ips = ["%s/32"]
307	
308	[log]
309	level = "error"
310	`, psk, concPaths.String(), concPriv, listenPort, edgePub, edgeInner))
311	
312		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg)
313		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", bin, "--config", edgeCfg)
314	
315		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
316			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
317		}
318		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
319			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
320		}
321		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
322		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
323		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
324		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
325		return edge, conc
326	}
327	
328	// scrapeMetrics is a one-shot scrape of url, failing the test on any error (used
329	// once the endpoint is already known to be up — unlike waitPathUp's tolerant poll).
330	func scrapeMetrics(t *testing.T, url string) metrics.Exposition {
331		t.Helper()
332		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 5*time.Second)
333		defer cancel()
334		exp, err := metrics.Fetch(ctx, http.DefaultClient, url)
335		if err != nil {
336			t.Fatalf("scrape %s: %v", url, err)
337		}
338		return exp
339	}
340	
341	// waitPathUp polls url's /metrics until the named path's wanbond_path_up series
342	// reads want, or fails the test at deadline. A scrape error is tolerated mid-poll
343	// (transient — never the terminal condition), since the assertion is about the
344	// SERIES VALUE appearing, not about the endpoint's own availability (already proven
345	// up by an earlier successful scrape in the caller).
346	func waitPathUp(t *testing.T, url, path string, want float64, deadline time.Duration) {
347		t.Helper()
348		end := time.Now().Add(deadline)
349		var lastErr error
350		for time.Now().Before(end) {
351			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 1*time.Second)
352			exp, err := metrics.Fetch(ctx, http.DefaultClient, url)
353			cancel()
354			if err != nil {
355				lastErr = err
356			} else if v, ok := exp.PathValue(metrics.MetricUp, path); ok && v == want {
357				return
358			}
359			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
360		}
361		t.Fatalf("wanbond_path_up{path=%q} never reached %v within %s (last scrape error: %v)", path, want, deadline, lastErr)
362	}

file.content:
### Series to watch

Exact exported series (labels/help verbatim from `internal/metrics/metrics.go`).
Every per-path series carries a `path="<name>"` label matching the `[[paths]]`
`name`:

| Series                                          | Meaning / what to watch |
|-------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| `wanbond_path_up{path}`                         | Per-path liveness, `1`=up `0`=down. **Primary health signal.** |
| `wanbond_path_throughput_bits_per_second{path}` | Current per-path throughput; confirms traffic is striping. |
| `wanbond_path_loss_ratio{path}`                 | Probe loss fraction `[0,1]`; rising loss precedes a path drop. |
| `wanbond_path_rtt_seconds{path}`                | Smoothed RTT; a jump signals congestion/bufferbloat. |
| `wanbond_path_jitter_seconds{path}`             | Smoothed RTT deviation. |
| `wanbond_path_tx_bytes_total{path}` / `wanbond_path_rx_bytes_total{path}` | Per-path byte counters. |
| `wanbond_fec_recovered_packets_total`           | DATA packets reconstructed by FEC (masked loss). |
| `wanbond_fec_unrecoverable_packets_total`       | DATA lost beyond FEC repair — **should stay near-flat**. |
| `wanbond_fec_residual_loss_ratio`               | Post-recovery connection loss `[0,1]`; compare to your `target_residual`. |
| `wanbond_fec_repair_packets_total` / `wanbond_fec_data_packets_total` | Parity vs data counts — the overhead ratio (FEC only). |

> **Rekey / handshake note.** wanbond exposes **no** rekey or handshake-age
> metric — the embedded amneziawg-go engine performs the Noise rekey internally
> and it is transparent to the bonding layer. Confirm rekey survival
> **operationally**: run a soak longer than one rekey interval and verify
> `wanbond_path_up` never drops and `wanbond_fec_unrecoverable_packets_total`
> does not step up across the rekey (see the reference soak in the appendix).
> The FEC series are emitted only when an `[fec]` block is configured.

### Basic health check

A pilot is healthy when the tunnel is up (**at least one** path up) and,
ideally, **every** configured path is up. Since "tunnel up" is the max of the
per-path liveness gauges, both conditions reduce to `wanbond_path_up`:

```sh
# Exit 0 iff every configured path reports up (=1). Prints each path's state.
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:9090/metrics \
  | awk '/^wanbond_path_up\{/ { print; if ($NF != 1) bad=1 }
         END { exit bad }' \
  && echo "OK: all paths up" || echo "DEGRADED: a path is down"
```

> metric — the embedded amneziawg-go engine performs the Noise rekey internally
> and it is transparent to the bonding layer. Confirm rekey survival

input.new_string:
| `wanbond_fec_repair_packets_total` / `wanbond_fec_data_packets_total` | Parity vs data counts — the overhead ratio (FEC only). |
| `wanbond_session_established`                    | WG session liveness, `1`=a handshake has completed and is still fresh, `0`=still converging **or** wedged. **Distinguishes "converging" from "wedged".** |
| `wanbond_session_last_handshake_seconds`         | Age of the peer's most recent completed WG handshake (`0` when none has completed). |

> **Session vs paths.** `wanbond_session_established` is the WG-session signal the
> per-path gauges cannot give you: a path can be `up` (probes reflect) while the
> Noise handshake has not yet completed (**converging**) or has aged out
> (**wedged**). Read it together with `wanbond_path_up` — a healthy tunnel is
> `wanbond_path_up=1` **and** `wanbond_session_established=1`; a path up with
> `wanbond_session_established=0` past the initial convergence window is the wedged
> case. The daemon also logs one INFO `session established` record on each `0→1`
> transition. `wanbond_session_last_handshake_seconds` grows between rekeys and
> resets on each completed handshake; a healthy tunnel keeps it well under the
> 180s validity window (rekey + edge keepalive refresh it), so a value that climbs
> toward 180s and drops `wanbond_session_established` to `0` is the wedged signal.
>
> The FEC series are emitted only when an `[fec]` block is configured.

WT=/home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-a0d7a77e637809a31; git -C "$WT" branch --show-current && git -C "$WT" add -A && git -C "$WT" commit -q -F - <<'EOF'
feat(metrics): wanbond_session_established + last-handshake age + 'session established' log (I2)

Add a WG-session signal to the metrics plane so operators can distinguish a
tunnel that is STILL CONVERGING (no completed Noise handshake) from one that is
WEDGED (a path is up but the handshake is absent or aged out) — the ambiguity
D35/D36/D37 all presented identically without.

- metrics.Source: new SessionSnapshot{Established, LastHandshakeAge} + Session()
  seam; collector registers/emits wanbond_session_established (0/1 gauge) and
  wanbond_session_last_handshake_seconds (age gauge). The bind stays WG-unaware.
- device.sessionMonitor reads the amneziawg engine's peer last-handshake via the
  UAPI GET seam (there is no public accessor for a peer's last-handshake instant),
  resolving the most-recent completed handshake across peers into the snapshot;
  Established requires the handshake to be within RejectAfterTime (fresh). It backs
  both the scrape-time /metrics snapshot and a probe-cadence poll loop that emits
  ONE INFO 'session established' record per 0->1 edge (sessionEdge detector).
- device.up wires the monitor into newMetricsSource and starts/stops the poll loop
  alongside the probe/reconcile/failover loops.

Tests: metric registration + established/not-established exposition (metrics);
snapshot parsing (established / never-handshaked / aged-out / most-recent-peer /
engine-error), the 0->1 edge detector with a steady-state mutation discriminator,
and the poll loop's once-per-session log (device). A deferred netns e2e
(session_established_test.go) asserts the 0->1 scrape transition, the
once-per-session record, and the path-up-before-session log-timestamp ordering;
it compiles + vets (execution deferred, G2 pattern).

Docs: runbook metrics table + the now-stale "exposes no handshake-age metric" note.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
git -C "$WT" log --oneline -1 && git -C "$WT" rev-parse HEAD

content.text:
Main checkout source is clean (my accidental stray edit was remediated), and the worktree is clean with the commit in place.

### Session summary
- **Did:** implemented task T101 in worktree `implement/T101` — added the `wanbond_session_established` gauge, `wanbond_session_last_handshake_seconds` age, and the one-per-session `session established` INFO log via a device-side edge detector.
- **Achieved:** status=pass; resultCommit `1e4955cd66c425aca7d0d63a9c3d147c3c800fe8`; full gate green (`go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e`, `go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e`, `go test ./...` all pass; gofmt clean). Mutation-verified the 0→1 edge discriminator (breaking `observe` to `return established` fails both edge tests).
- **Discovered:** the amneziawg-go `Peer.lastHandshakeNano` field is private with no public accessor, so the last-handshake instant must be read via `dev.IpcGet()` UAPI-text parsing (not a `LookupPeer` field load); `awgdevice.RejectAfterTime` (180s) is the natural freshness window. The runbook carried a now-false "wanbond exposes no handshake-age metric" note — updated it and the metrics table (doc-sync rule).
- **Issues:** **Process error worth flagging** — early Bash commands ran with `cd /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia` (the MAIN checkout) instead of the worktree, so one `sed` accidentally mutated the main checkout's `internal/device/metrics_test.go`. I remediated it with `git -C <main> checkout -- internal/device/metrics_test.go` (restoring only the single file I wrongly touched; no refs/commits affected) and re-did all work inside the worktree. Main checkout `internal/`/`docs/`/`test/` is now clean. The netns e2e is compile-only/deferred (G2 pattern) — its path-up-before-session ordering assertion rests on the design premise that wanbond's probe plane declares path-up before the WG handshake fires on inner traffic/keepalive; that ordering needs hardware validation when the e2e tier runs.
